message:
feat(fj-interpreters): execute functional scan sub-jaxprs (frankenjax-u0y4)

Adds interpreter-level functional scan execution to fj-interpreters,
mirroring the BatchTrace functional-scan handling already landed in
fj-dispatch and the [body] sub_jaxpr convention documented in fj-core.

Implementation (crates/fj-interpreters/src/lib.rs)
- Adds evaluate_scan_sub_jaxprs handling Primitive::Scan with one body
  sub_jaxpr, supporting the [const_values..., carry..., xs] binding
  layout and the [carry..., ys...] output layout used by fj-core/lax.
- Honors the reverse=true equation parameter by iterating xs in reverse
  while preserving original ordering of accumulated ys per fj-core
  semantics (avoids the silent gradient-routing trap from the related
  switch AD work).
- Validates body arity, carry/ys split, output count, and produces
  actionable InterpreterError variants for the empty-xs / abstract-shape
  edge case rather than silently returning empty ys.

Tests
- eval_scan_with_sub_jaxprs_executes_carry_and_ys
- eval_scan_with_sub_jaxprs_reverse_preserves_input_order_ys
- eval_scan_with_sub_jaxprs_handles_multi_carry_and_ys
  Cover forward + reverse scans, multi-carry composition, and the
  ys ordering invariants that BatchTrace relies on.

Beads
- Adds frankenjax-u0y4 (in_progress) so the work is tracked while the
  remaining fj-interpreters parity surface (zero-length functional scan
  outputs requiring abstract shapes) is finished in a follow-up.

Docs
- Bumps README test count badge / table from 2,186 to 2,189 to reflect
  the three new fj-interpreters scan tests added here.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
